oil_supply_shock_driver.note: Hormuz flows ~4M bpd (vs normal 16M); Brent peaked $126/bbl; EIA Q2 2026 avg $91/bbl; 5yr breakeven +26bps since conflict onset; Fed and ECB both citing energy as primary inflation driver
oil_supply_shock_driver.resolution_criteria: AIS-confirmed resumption of commercial crossings; geopolitical premium drops below $5/bbl

version_history.change: Initial driver set to Iran conflict / Hormuz restriction
version_history.reason: Zero AIS crossings confirmed by Windward Maritime Intelligence

version_history.change: Oil shock materially escalated: Brent peaked $126/bbl, Hormuz flows at 25% of normal, Gulf producers cutting 10M bpd
version_history.reason: EIA STEO March 2026; JPMorgan; Reuters; intellectia.ai — confirmed largest supply disruption in history
version_history.prior_value: $15–20 USD/barrel geopolitical premium; zero AIS commercial crossings as of March 14, 2026

version_history.reason: Indicator reading +12.5% Q1 est. but structurally suppressed

blind_spot_overrides.warning: Nominal M2 expansion is thinning in real terms. Forward Real M2 may turn negative by Q3 2026.

conviction_history.rationale: All eight asset classes negative; zero bullish scores; stagflation confirmed by growth and inflation data

All 8 asset classes negative (issue 2). No tactical alerts (all deltas < 0.45). Liberation Day tariffs, private credit gate events, consumer confidence 53.3 all confirmed. EM Equities improved +0.19 on FX swap basis but Liberation Day tariffs likely to reverse this.

All 8 asset classes negative; 6 BEARISH, 2 MILD NEGATIVE. Liberation Day tariff cascade T3 active; dollar_weaponization and treasury_market_liquidity upgraded to WARNING. System average crossed CRITICAL threshold (<-0.50).

tariff_escalation_protocol.activation_reason: Liberation Day tariff package — effective US tariff rate exceeds 15%, disputes active with EU/CN/CA/MX simultaneously
tariff_escalation_protocol.current_rung_basis: Enacted + active retaliation by multiple major partners

imf_weo_status.note: IMF WEO April 2026 expected mid-April. When published: update SA indicators, check fragile frontier sovereigns, assess tariff growth impact.

blind_spots.name: Tariff-driven earnings beats
blind_spots.description: Companies with US domestic revenue reporting beats due to tariff shelter, not operational improvement. Apply during active tariff protocol to Tech and Consumer Staples.

blind_spots.name: European defence spending as growth signal
blind_spots.description: Defence spending on imported platforms has limited EU growth multiplier. Disaggregate European vs. US/imported procurement before scoring as macro positive.

blind_spots.name: Tariff-era earnings season distortion
blind_spots.description: Q1 earnings pre-date Liberation Day impact. Pre-tariff beats may appear in early April reports. Guidance revisions for Q2 are the real signal. Do not upgrade earnings_revisions from GREEN until Q2 guidance season is complete.

_challenge.justification: Two hard flags on fabricated Turnberry deal and regime logic, plus soft flags on blind spots and sources warrant human review before publication.

findings.quoted_claim: EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in 15 percent rate on EU exports reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness
findings.finding_preliminary: No evidence of any EU-US Turnberry tariff deal exists in web searches as of 2026-04-21. Turnberry refers to a Trump golf resort, not a known trade agreement, and the cited ING Think article discusses general tariff impacts without mentioning Turnberry or a specific 15 percent locked rate deal.

evidence.relevance: Article projects EU export slowdown to US at 4.6 percent in 2026 due to tariffs but does not mention Turnberry deal or locking in 15 percent rate

evidence.source: Web search for Turnberry tariff deal
evidence.relevance: No results for EU-US Turnberry tariff deal; Turnberry associated with Trump properties, not trade policy

findings.finding_preliminary: Stress regime remains CRISIS with Stable delta, but guardrails require corroborating evidence across at least two domains for any non-Stable delta; here delta is Stable yet lead signal and multiple domains cite new Turnberry deal as structural entrenchment without prior brief evidence, violating conjunctivity rule implicitly by introducing unverified driver.

findings.quoted_claim: earnings_revisions flag: ELEVATED, EPS revision breadth -15 percent, source: FactSet
findings.finding_preliminary: Earnings_revisions flagged ELEVATED (not GREEN), so Blind Spot 1 trigger does not apply, but Tech asset_class lacks any caveat on buyback distortion despite guardrail expectation for GREEN cases; FactSet data unverifiable for exact -15 percent without URL.

findings.quoted_claim: m2_money_supply flag: ELEVATED direction: DETERIORATING value: M2 YoY growth 2.1 percent
findings.finding_preliminary: Nominal M2 at 2.1 percent YoY triggers Blind Spot 2 for Crypto (flag ELEVATED + DETERIORATING), and Crypto has banking distrust warning but lacks specific real M2 caveat as required; no T1/T2 source confirms exact 2.1 percent for week ending 2026-04-18.

evidence.source: Federal Reserve H.6 Money Stock Measures
evidence.relevance: Latest H.6 shows M2 YoY around 1.8-2.0 percent as of mid-April 2026, close but requires verification; real M2 negative per CPI adjustment

findings.quoted_claim: trump_tariffs current_driver: USMCA review and Section 232 enforcement
findings.finding_preliminary: Tariff_tracker cites 2026-04-02 effective dates unverifiable; prior brief mentions Section 122 and Yale estimates at 9.6-11 percent, not 17.2 percent effective rate without T1 source.

evidence.relevance: Prior reports effective tariff 11.0 percent, highest since 1943

findings.quoted_claim: Energy blind_spot_warning: Energy equity vs commodity divergence blind spot active Hormuz driver unresolved
findings.finding_preliminary: Acknowledges documented false-positive 4 (energy commodity vs equity) and Hormuz-flip risk per guardrails 6.1, but prior briefs describe Hormuz closure as ongoing post-ceasefire with persistent disruption, not resolved.

evidence.relevance: Hormuz traffic far below pre-war levels post-April 8 ceasefire
